name: spex-machina
description: The spec state machine. A standalone CLI that owns the structural half of spec-driven development: typed graph specs, merkle-based change tracking, deterministic impact analysis, and bead task mapping.

requirements.title: Validate spec structure
requirements.description: Given a spec directory, confirm it is a valid DAG with no orphans, no cycles, no broken references. Structured error output.

requirements.title: Compute merkle tree
requirements.description: Hash every node, store snapshots, diff against previous snapshots, classify change impact paths.

requirements.title: Map spec nodes to beads
requirements.description: Each plan-relevant spec node maps to a bead with spec_id, typed dependencies, and full metadata (requirements, component, impl_section, module, spec_hash, proposal).

requirements.title: Compute impact
requirements.description: Given changed spec nodes, find all affected beads and classify actions (create/close/review).

requirements.title: Apply changes
requirements.description: Execute bead actions via bead CLI (br or bd), tag with proposal, save snapshot.

requirements.title: Manage proposals
requirements.description: Register proposals, validate structure, link to spec changes and bead actions, show history.

requirements.title: Render spec
requirements.description: Generate markdown, DOT, or JSON from spec structure.

requirements.title: Self-hosting
requirements.description: Spex Machina's own spec is managed by Spex Machina (after bootstrap).

requirements.title: Deterministic
requirements.description: Given the same spec state and snapshot, always produce the same diff, impact, and actions. No LLM calls.

requirements.title: Composable
requirements.description: Every subcommand reads stdin or files, writes stdout or files, exits 0/1. Pipeable.

requirements.title: Fast
requirements.description: Validating a spec with 100 modules and 1000 nodes should take less than 1 second.

requirements.title: Git-native
requirements.description: Snapshots are files committed to git. Proposals are files committed to git. No external state.

modules.name: Schema
modules.description: JSON Schema definitions for project.json and module.json. The foundation that defines what a valid spec looks like.

modules.name: Validator
modules.description: Spec directory validation: JSON schema conformance, content path resolution, DAG acyclicity, orphan detection, ID uniqueness.

modules.name: Merkle
modules.description: Merkle hash tree over the spec: leaf hashes, interior hashes, snapshots, diff, and impact classification.

modules.name: Impact
modules.description: Maps merkle diff to affected beads: reads bead metadata, matches changed nodes, classifies actions (create/close/review).

modules.name: Apply
modules.description: Executes impact report: creates/closes/updates beads via bead CLI (br or bd), tags with proposal, saves new merkle snapshot.

modules.name: Proposal
modules.description: Proposal lifecycle management: registration, structure validation, history tracking, templates.

modules.name: Render
modules.description: Generates human-readable output from spec: collated markdown, graphviz DOT, machine-readable JSON.

milestones.title: Bootstrap
milestones.description: Core schema and validation working. Spec can be authored and validated.

milestones.title: Change Tracking
milestones.description: Merkle tree and impact analysis. Spec changes detected and mapped to beads.

milestones.title: Automation
milestones.description: Full pipeline: spec change → impact → bead actions executed automatically.

milestones.title: Lifecycle
milestones.description: Proposal management and spec rendering for human consumption.
